seo.title: 15 Best Things To Do in Chumphon (2026 Guide) — Diving, Beaches & Islands
seo.metaDescription: Discover the 15 best things to do in Chumphon in 2026. From Chumphon Pinnacle diving to Koh Tao ferries, Hat Thung Wua Laen Beach, and seafood markets. Real prices and insider tips.

intro: Chumphon is more than a ferry stop for Koh Tao — it's a low-key Gulf coast province with some of Thailand's least-crowded beaches, top-tier dive sites, a genuine fishing culture, and jungle hot springs. Most travelers rush through on overnight trains and miss it entirely. That's their loss. If you spend two or three days, Chumphon reveals itself as one of southern Thailand's most authentic coastal escapes. Here are 15 things that make it worth the stop.

activities.name: Dive Chumphon Pinnacle
activities.description: One of Thailand's great dive sites — an underwater seamount 23 km offshore that rises from 36 meters to within 14 meters of the surface. Massive aggregations of big-eye trevally, barracuda, and giant barracuda circle the pinnacle in silvery walls. Whale sharks and leopard sharks appear regularly between March and May. Visibility often reaches 20-25 meters in the dry season. Because most divers head straight to Koh Tao, the Pinnacle sees far fewer boats than its reputation warrants. PADI dive shops at Hat Thung Wua Laen offer 2-dive day trips.
activities.price: 2,500-3,500 THB for a 2-dive day trip with gear
tips: Advanced Open Water certification is recommended — currents can be moderate to strong
March-May offers the best whale shark encounters and peak visibility
Book with PADI-certified operators at Hat Thung Wua Laen for English-speaking divemasters

activities.name: Relax at Hat Thung Wua Laen Beach
activities.description: Chumphon's crown jewel — a 3-km stretch of wide white sand 20 km north of town, backed by coconut palms and almost entirely free of crowds. The sea is calm and clear from November to May, perfect for swimming. A small strip of beachfront guesthouses, seafood restaurants, and dive shops sits behind the sand. Rent a beach chair and a fresh coconut and enjoy the unhurried pace that's disappeared from more famous Thai beaches.
activities.price: Free (beach chairs 50-100 THB, food 150-400 THB per person)
tips: Rent a motorbike in town (200-250 THB/day) — there's no reliable public transport to the beach
The northern end of the beach is quietest — walk past the main cluster of restaurants
Beachfront seafood restaurants serve the daily catch at excellent prices — try the grilled fish (pla pao) for 200-350 THB

activities.name: Take the Ferry to Koh Tao
activities.description: Chumphon is the closest mainland departure point for Koh Tao — Thailand's world-famous diving island. Lomprayah's high-speed catamaran departs from Lomprayah Pier (7 km from town) at 7:00 AM and 1:30 PM, arriving at Mae Haad Pier, Koh Tao in 1 hour 45 minutes. The journey crosses the Gulf of Thailand with views of the mainland coast and distant islands. Buy tickets at the Lomprayah town office on Tha Tapao Road or book online at lomprayah.com.
tips: The 7:00 AM departure is popular — book ahead in peak season (December-February)
The Lomprayah shuttle bus from their town office to the pier costs 50 THB and departs 90 minutes before sailing
Combo tickets to Koh Phangan and Koh Samui are available and save money over separate tickets
activities.bestTime: Year-round (occasional cancellations August-September in rough weather)

activities.name: Snorkel the Islands of Mu Ko Chumphon National Park
activities.description: Day trips from Pak Nam Chumphon pier to the national park's 40+ islands, including Ko Ngam Yai and Ko Ngam Noi — twin islands with white-sand beaches and excellent snorkeling over healthy coral reefs. Ko Talu is known for its crystal-clear water and diverse marine life. Ko Lawa and Ko Mattra are popular dive sites with hard coral gardens starting at 5 meters. The park sees far fewer visitors than the Samui or Phi Phi island groups.
activities.price: 800-1,500 THB boat tour + 200 THB national park fee
tips: Book day trips through guesthouses in Chumphon town or directly at Pak Nam pier
Bring your own snorkel gear if you have it — rentals are basic quality from boat operators
Ko Ngam Noi has a sandy beach suitable for swimming; Ko Talu is better for snorkeling

activities.name: Explore Pak Nam Chumphon Seafood Market
activities.description: The estuary fishing village 11 km south of town where the Chumphon River meets the Gulf — Chumphon's authentic heart. Fishing boats unload their catch twice daily and small quayside restaurants fry or grill it immediately. Blue crab (pu nue) is the local specialty — Chumphon crab is sold at premium prices throughout Thailand for its sweetness and size. Watch boats return at dawn, bargain for fresh seafood, and eat a crab lunch for a fraction of Bangkok prices.
activities.price: Free to explore (food 150-400 THB per person)
tips: Morning (7-9 AM) is most atmospheric — fishing boats returning with the night's catch
Steamed whole blue crab costs 200-400 THB per kg depending on size — split one between two people
The pier is the departure point for national park island day trips — you can combine a morning market visit with an afternoon island snorkeling trip
activities.bestTime: Morning (7-9 AM) or late afternoon (4-6 PM)

activities.name: Visit Wat Phra Borommathat Chumphon
activities.description: Chumphon's most important temple complex in Sawi district, 45 km south of town. The site is dominated by a tall white chedi (stupa) believed to enshrine a sacred Buddha relic, with origins in the Srivijaya Empire of the 8th century. The complex is a major pilgrimage site for Thai Buddhists — expect incense smoke, elaborate offerings, and a contemplative atmosphere. The surrounding town of Sawi has excellent local seafood restaurants.
tips: Dress modestly — cover shoulders and knees; sarongs are often available at the entrance
The temple complex is busiest on Buddhist holidays (Visakha Bucha, Makha Bucha) and weekends
Combine with a stop in Sawi town for lunch — the riverside seafood restaurants are excellent and cheaper than Chumphon town
activities.bestTime: Weekday mornings for a peaceful experience

activities.name: Soak in Tham Khao Phlu Hot Spring
activities.description: A jungle hot spring near the town of Lang Suan, 80 km south of Chumphon, consisting of three natural thermal pools at around 38-42C year-round. The warm mineral-rich water is set in a forested valley with a small waterfall nearby. Far less visited than Krabi's more famous hot springs, Tham Khao Phlu is a genuine local secret — you'll likely share it with a few Thai families rather than tour groups.
tips: Rent a motorbike or hire a songthaew from Lang Suan town — no public transport runs directly here
Bring a swimsuit and towel; changing rooms are basic
The pools are warmest in the morning before the sun heats the surrounding air
activities.bestTime: Year-round (cooler months are most enjoyable for hot soaking)

activities.name: Hike to Khao Matsi Viewpoint
activities.description: A short but rewarding hilltop hike southeast of Chumphon town to a 360-degree panoramic viewpoint. The summit looks out over the Chumphon River estuary and its fishing pier communities, the wide sweep of Hat Pharadon Phap beach running south along the Gulf, and on clear days the distant silhouettes of offshore islands. A forested trail leads to the summit — good for birdwatching on the way up.
tips: Start early morning or late afternoon to avoid midday heat — there's little shade on the upper section
The trail from the road to the summit takes 30-40 minutes at a comfortable pace
Bring binoculars for birdwatching and to spot fishing boats in the estuary below
activities.bestTime: Early morning for sunrise or late afternoon for sunset

activities.name: Eat at Chumphon Night Market
activities.description: The daily evening market on Kromluang Chumphon Road near the Chumphon town center, running from around 5 PM to 9 PM. This is the cheapest and most authentic place to eat in the province. Local vendors sell grilled seafood skewers, khao man gai (Hainanese chicken rice), som tam (papaya salad), kuay teow (noodle soup), roti, and fresh fruit shakes. The market has genuine local atmosphere with little tourist traffic.
activities.price: 150-300 THB for a full meal per person (~$4-8 USD)
tips: Look for the stalls with the longest Thai queues — those are the ones worth trying
Chumphon-style grilled fish (pla pao) is a local specialty — whole fish packed with lemongrass and wrapped in banana leaf before grilling
Fresh coconut juice (ma phrao) is sold at several stalls for 20-30 THB — perfect for the heat

activities.name: Kitesurfing at Hat Pharadon Phap
activities.description: A 14-km-long undeveloped beach south of Chumphon town, virtually deserted outside of local fishermen and occasional kitesurfers. The beach fronts the Gulf with steady onshore winds from November to February making it one of southern Thailand's best kitesurfing spots. The sand is wide and flat, perfect for kitesurfing launches, and the water depth is suitable for beginners. One kite school operates seasonally at the north end of the beach.
activities.price: Kitesurfing lessons 2,500-4,000 THB for half-day beginner course
tips: Beginner kite lessons run November to February when winds are most consistent (15-25 knots)
The beach is free to access — just walk or ride to the sand without a kite for a quiet swimming spot
A motorbike from town (200-250 THB/day) is the only practical way to reach the beach
activities.bestTime: November to February for kitesurfing; year-round for beach walking

activities.name: Day Trip to Ko Talu for Snorkeling
activities.description: Ko Talu is one of the most pristine islands in the Chumphon archipelago — crystal-clear water, abundant coral fish, and almost no permanent habitation. The island sits within Mu Ko Chumphon National Park and has several dive and snorkel sites starting at just 3-5 meters depth, ideal for beginners. Longtail boat day trips from Pak Nam Chumphon take 40-60 minutes each way.
activities.price: 1,200-1,800 THB including boat, snorkel gear, and national park fee
tips: Book through guesthouses in Chumphon town or ask at Pak Nam pier for group day trip departures
Bring reef-safe sunscreen — many operators request this to protect the coral
The water around Ko Talu is excellent for both snorkeling and diving — PADI operators run dive trips to the island

activities.name: Kayak Through Chumphon Mangroves
activities.description: The Chumphon River estuary and the coastline around Pak Nam Chumphon are fringed with mangrove forests — a sheltered and scenic environment for sea kayaking. Paddle through mangrove channels inhabited by monitor lizards, mudskippers, fishing birds, and occasionally otters. Several guesthouses in Pak Nam rent kayaks or organize guided half-day tours. The calm estuary water makes this suitable for all ability levels.
activities.price: 200-400 THB kayak rental; guided tours 600-900 THB
tips: Morning paddling (before 10 AM) offers the best wildlife sightings and cooler temperatures
The mangrove channels are best explored at high tide — ask locals or your guesthouse about tide times
Bring insect repellent — mangrove areas can have mosquitoes, especially in the evening
activities.bestTime: Year-round (sheltered estuary not affected by monsoon seas)

activities.name: Visit Prince Chumphon Shrine
activities.description: The memorial shrine dedicated to Admiral Prince Chumphon (Prince Abhakara Kiartiwongse), the Father of the Royal Thai Navy, stands prominently at the seafront near Pak Nam Chumphon. The prince died in 1923 and is deeply revered in the province — his name is given to the city itself. Locals leave offerings of incense and flowers at the ornate golden statue. The surrounding seafront area is pleasant for a morning or evening walk.
tips: Visit in the morning when Thai locals come to pay respects with offerings
The shrine is right on the waterfront — combine with a seafood breakfast at the nearby Pak Nam restaurants
The statue is particularly striking lit up in the evening hours

activities.name: Watch the Sunrise at Hat Arunothai
activities.description: A secluded beach 15 km north of Chumphon town, even quieter than Hat Thung Wua Laen, facing southeast across the Gulf of Thailand. Because the beach faces east, it catches the sunrise directly — the sky turns from deep orange to vivid pink as the sun rises over the open water. Almost no tourist infrastructure — just a quiet stretch of sand, fishing boats, and occasional locals. Perfect for photographers and early risers.
tips: Check sunrise times online — roughly 6:30-7:00 AM year-round in southern Thailand
Ride out from town the evening before for a seafood dinner on the beach, then return for sunrise the next morning
The beach is accessible by motorbike — the road from Chumphon town to Hat Arunothai is well-paved and takes 25 minutes

activities.name: Explore Chumphon Town's Local Market
activities.description: Chumphon's municipal market near the intersection of Sala Daeng Road and Tha Tapao Road is one of the most genuine local markets in southern Thailand. Two levels of stalls sell fresh produce, dried seafood, local snacks, and everyday goods at prices that serve working Thais rather than tourists. Look for dried shrimp (kung haeng), fermented fish paste (kapi), and fresh tropical fruits. The market is busiest from 6-10 AM when locals buy breakfast and groceries.
tips: Come early — the market is most active between 6 AM and 10 AM
Try the local kanom jeen (fermented rice noodles served with spicy southern Thai curry sauce) from market vendors — 30-40 THB per bowl
Dried seafood stalls at the market sell Chumphon's famous dried squid (pla muek haeng) — an excellent souvenir or train snack

travelTips: A motorbike rental (200-250 THB/day) is the single most useful thing you can organize in Chumphon — beaches, viewpoints, and Pak Nam are all beyond comfortable walking distance
The Chumphon Pinnacle is accessible on day trips from Hat Thung Wua Laen — if you're a diver, consider staying a day or two rather than rushing to Koh Tao
Hat Thung Wua Laen has the best beachfront restaurant strip — arrive for lunch, swim in the afternoon, and watch sunset with a cold Singha beer
All ferry departures to Koh Tao use Lomprayah Pier, 7 km from town — take the Lomprayah shuttle bus (50 THB) rather than a tuk-tuk to guarantee timing
